title: ENGINE REMOVAL

figures_detected.description: Photograph showing engine bay with engine removal setup, slingers and mounting points visible

content_blocks.text:
WARNING:
• Situate vehicle on a flat and solid surface.
• Place chocks at front and back of rear wheels.
• Do not remove engine until exhaust system has completely cooled down.
• For safety during subsequent steps, the tension of wires should be slackened against the engine.
• Before removing front axle from transaxle, place safety stands under designated front supporting points. Refer to GI section for lifting points and towing.
• Be sure to hoist engine and transaxle in a safe manner.
• For engines not equipped with engine slingers, attach proper slingers and bolts described in PARTS CATALOG or Eurofast.

CAUTION:
• When lifting engine, be careful not to strike adjacent parts, especially accelerator wire casing, brake lines, and brake master cylinder.
• Always use engine slingers when hoisting the engine.
• When removing drive shaft, be careful not to damage transaxle oil seal.

steps.text: Remove engine undercovers and splash guards.
Drain coolant from both radiator and engine.
Disconnect all coolant hoses from engine.
Disconnect fuel tubes and vacuum tubes.
Release drive belt adjusting bolts and remove power steering pump from engine. Bind pump properly to the vehicle.
Remove the battery and the battery tray.
Disconnect or remove electrical wiring where necessary.
Disconnect clutch lever cable and accelerator cable.
Disconnect speedometer and neutral switch connector from transaxle housing.
Remove brake caliper mounting bolts and bind caliper properly to vehicle LH & RH.
Disconnect tie rod ball joints and lower ball joints (LH & RH).
Mount suitable slingers to cylinder head. Use a suitable hoist to release the force on the engine mountings.

model_notes: LH & RH distinctions noted for brake calipers and ball joints. Procedure applies to engine/transaxle assembly removal. Power steering pump removal step implies power steering equipped variant.
